articles.title: Neither Farm Nor Hotel: Why Agritourism Needs Its Own Architectural Typology
articles.summary: This article is part of our new Opinion section, a format for argument-driven essays on critical questions shaping our field.

articles.title: Museum of Humanity / studio apaaar
articles.summary: A journey showcasing love, the inferiority complex it brought, and how I overcame it. It all started when society brutally declared that expressing love was a 'sin'. An individual suddenly hands you a clean pair of glasses, changes your perspective, just to say, "Love isn't a sin; it's a natural sentiment", giving me a new vision to appreciate art.

articles.title: Forest of Knowledge Library / studio hinge
articles.summary: Going beyond books, the library is reimagined as a forest of knowledge, bringing people together to foster learning in a space inspired by the canopy of foliage in the adjacent street, reminiscent of reading under a tree.

articles.title: Made to Measure: Herzog & de Meuron’s Architectural Approach to Furniture Design
articles.summary: What allows a single architectural principle to generate many different outcomes? The answer lies in the systems that organize architecture: geometry, proportion, structure, and construction. Together, these can establish a set of rules that can accommodate different programs, contexts, and spatial conditions while maintaining a coherent identity. A structural grid, for example, can organize a variety of uses, just as a single construction detail can be repeated throughout an entire building without producing identical spaces. Can the same way of thinking be translated into furniture design wi

articles.title: REDGE Contemporary Art Center  / Atelier Meadow
articles.summary: As contemporary buildings become obsolete faster than cities can regenerate them, REDGE Contemporary Art Center explores adaptive reuse as a strategy not only for retaining existing buildings, but for extending the life of the recent built environment. Designed by Atelier Meadow under the direction of Edoardo Nieri, REDGE Contemporary Art Center transforms the first three floors of an office tower completed less than a decade ago into a public cultural institution in Chengdu , China. Commissioned as part of the development of Chengdu Tianfu International Biotown, the project introduces exhibit

articles.title: Martha Beatriz House / Atempo Arquitectura
articles.summary: Martha Beatriz House is a renovation and expansion project of a residence located in a residential neighborhood of the Historic Center of Cuenca . The house is named after Martha Beatriz Cardoso Faicán, the project's developer, who from the beginning shared the conviction that the value of a building depends not only on its heritage status, but also on the spatial, material, and emotional qualities it preserves.

articles.title: Ponta Fina House - Aiuruoca / Gil Mello Arquitetura
articles.summary: The design of Sítio da Ponta Fina, located in Aiuruoca, Minas Gerais, revives the occupation of a plateau on the slopes of the Serra do Papagaio, introducing a main house alongside complementary structures for support, leisure, work, and guest accommodation.

articles.title: Casa Óculo / OHLAB
articles.summary: Casa Óculo is a single-family house located in a residential neighborhood on the outskirts of Palma . Defined by a simple yet bold architectural gesture – a large flat roof that stretches from east to west – the project uses light, materiality, and climate as its primary design tools. The clear, rational form responds to a south-facing slope, open mountain views, and a client with a structured program, resulting in a contemporary Mediterranean home that balances efficiency, tactility, and a deep sense of belonging.

articles.title: Knowledge Arena / SENAC São Paulo + Estudio Guto Requena
articles.summary: Architecture, sustainability, and education come together to create an experience of welcoming, lingering, and exchange. Developed by faculty and students from the Architecture and Urbanism program at Centro Universitário Senac, alongside Estudio Guto Requena, the project encourages a reflection on the connections between people, knowledge, and space.

articles.title: Racine Carrée Social Housing / rue royale architectes
articles.summary: Racine Carrée draws its identity from the convergence of a structured approach – inspired by the spatial permutations of a Rubik's Cube – and a sensitive connection to the natural landscape. The project is based on the rigorous development of an orderly facade grid, set in tension with the site's inherent qualities. It thus expresses a fundamental conceptual duality between geometric rationality and integration into the landscape.

articles.title: John Meng's Found-Object Cigar Ashtray
articles.summary: This Achille cigar ashtray is by Zhangzhou-based designer John Meng. "A cigar ashtray constructed from espresso portafilter component, a filter basket, and a blind bowl. Stainless steel and Beech wood. Hand-polished." "A subtle balance between individuality and elegance is always the goal - an ashtray should be unmistakably itself, yet never loud. It should hold its place in a room not by demanding attention, but by earning it — through its weight, its silence, and its permanence, year after year." Joe Colombo, who was known for designing vice-centric objects , would've dug this.

articles.title: An Apple Vision Pro Seating Station at a Parisian Opera House
articles.summary: Typically at an opera house, the only thing patrons are looking through are opera glasses. But at Paris' historic Palais Garnier, they've got Apple Vision Pro headsets on offer. This month the opera house launched a VR experience called " Dans les pas d'une Étoile " ("In the footsteps of a Star"), which lets visitors follow ballet star Bleuenn Battistoni through backstage spaces that are typically off-limits to the public. To view it, visitors sit at a circular banquette that has been outfitted with aluminum arms holding Apple Vision Pro headsets. The film was shot in 180°, and the headsets al

articles.title: Announcing Fusion Forward: Design for Home, a New Competition from Core77 and Autodesk Fusion
articles.summary: Core77 and Autodesk Fusion have teamed up to launch Fusion Forward: Design for Home , a competition inviting designers to reconsider the products that shape everyday domestic life. The brief is broad but pointed: create a consumer product for the home that shows real thought in material selection, form development, and utility. Judges want to see designs that don't just look good in a render but hold up to how people actually live, work, and take care of themselves at home. Entrants choose one of three categories: Smart Home Devices — controllers, sensors, displays, hubs, switches, and monitor

articles.title: Clever Design for a Stud Finder Combines Low and Hi Tech
articles.summary: The C.H. Hanson Magnetic Stud Finder is a simple, low-tech piece of kit. It has two powerful magnets on the back, and you wave it around a wall until it snaps to a screw behind the paint. Once I've got it stuck in place, I then use a separate laser level to project a vertical line aligned with the stud finder. Inventor Ruvim Kubsky does the Hanson one better. His Magnetic Stud Finder comes with a little laser dangling from the magnet on a pivot. Once the object is stuck fast to the wall, gravity brings the laser plumb. A simple, elegant combination of low and hi tech.
